content.problemStatement: You have N sticks, each with a certain length. You need to pair them up to make as many chopstick pairs as possible. The rule is: two sticks can form a pair if the difference in their lengths is at most D. A stick can only be part of one pair.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The 5 sticks have lengths 1, 3, 3, 9 and 4 respectively. The maximum allowed difference in the lengths of two sticks forming a pair is at most 2. It is clear that the 4th stick (length 9) cannot be used with any other stick. The remaining 4 sticks can can be paired as (1st and 3rd) and (2nd and 5th) to form 2 pairs of usable chopsticks.
